response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. Their professional C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (~7 months) plus academic work, far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. Their career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with no SQL Server/Oracle depth and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain experience. The closest financial exposure (RIDE Capital) was a Node/GraphQL fintech product, not investment banking systems.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience across web, backend, embedded, and low-latency systems
Proven high-performance systems work (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby) and strong CI/CD, testing, and craftsmanship practices
Excellent communication skills evidenced by workshops, conference talks, and teaching
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience and none recent, versus a mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain
Strong general engineering and communication skills
